title: [BUG] The WorkbookWriter() function in ExcelJS is overwriting RichText objects in multiple cells with the first RichText object.
author: techiesalman

comments.author: techiesalman
Siemienik
comments.body: Thanks fot investing this It seems like comparing these cells by string values (toString) except rich text settings.

comments.author: HelloYie
comments.body: +1， This seems to be a bug.

comments.author: belanEu
comments.body:
Hello!
Any updates on that bug?

comments.author: belanEu
comments.body: When option `useSharedStrings: false` is set, text gets changed, but for some reason there are no richText styles applied in the result xlsx file.

comments.author: belanEu
comments.body:
@Siemienik Hi!
I see that PetrChalov has pushed some fixes. I'm wondering if those changes are going to be merged soon. 👉👈

comments.author: belanEu
comments.body: Also to be mentioned about the case I've written before: I noticed that richText styles are ignored when `useSharedStrings: false` and `WorkbookWriter` is used. When `Workbook` is used, richText styles are applied as expected.

comments.author: protobi-pieter
comments.body: For anyone hitting this on a current release: the protobi fork has cherry-picked a fix as [protobi/exceljs#66](https://github.com/protobi/exceljs/pull/66), with regression tests. Upstream PR #2588 also addresses this bug but has been stale since Feb 2024.
